title: Airtable field name mismatch
symptom: API call to create or update records fails with 'UNKNOWN_FIELD_NAME'. The field exists visually in the Airtable base but the API rejects it.
applies_when: Creating or updating records via Airtable API
Field was recently renamed or has special characters
not_this_if: Field does not exist in the table at all
Error is about the table name, not field name
root_cause: Airtable API uses exact field names as they appear in the table header, including case sensitivity and special characters. A common mistake is using the field's internal ID instead of its display name, or using a stale cached name after renaming a field.
fix_steps: Open the Airtable base and verify the exact field name (case-sensitive)
Check for hidden characters: copy the field name directly from Airtable
Use the Airtable API 'list fields' endpoint to get exact field names: GET /v0/meta/bases/{baseId}/tables
Update your code/config to use the exact field name string
agent_instruction: Verify the exact field name (case-sensitive) in Airtable. Use the metadata API (GET /v0/meta/bases/{baseId}/tables) to list all field names. Update the API call to use the exact name.
safety_notes: Do not log Airtable API keys in error output
